visuals.title: Lecture Note: Integrability and BIBO Stability

images.caption: The note introduces absolute integrability and starts the BIBO argument.
The final inequality shows how bounded input and finite integral force bounded output.

visuals.title: Absolutely Integrable vs Non-Integrable Impulse Responses
visuals.description: A decaying impulse response has finite total area. A non-decaying one does not.

visuals.title: The BIBO Bound from Convolution
visuals.description: If |x(t)| is bounded by M and the area under |h(t)| is finite, the output stays bounded by their product.

visuals.title: Lecture Note: Final BIBO Stability Inequality
visuals.caption: The final line of the note states the engineering conclusion: if h(t) is absolutely integrable, the system is BIBO stable.
